🐾 PawSafePlants
Cat-Safe Plants 🌸
Toxic Plants 🚫
Moderate Risk ⚠️
Latest Stories 💕Safe Bouquets 🌸Blog 📝More Products 🎀

🚫 Toxic Plants for Cats

⚠️ If your cat comes into contact with these plants, seek veterinary care immediately!
❌ Highly toxic! Even small amounts can cause vomiting, kidney failure, or death
Plant NameCommon NameRisk Level
LilyLily⚠️ Extremely dangerous to cats! All parts (flowers, leaves, pollen, water) can cause acute kidney failure
OleanderOleanderCardiac toxin, even tiny amounts can be fatal
Sago PalmSago PalmSeeds are most toxic, can cause liver failure
TulipsTulipsBulbs are most toxic, cause drooling, seizures, irregular heartbeat
🚨 Emergency: If your cat contacts any of these plants, contact your veterinarian or pet poison control immediately!
🐱
Aloe Vera
⚠️
While aloe gel is safe for human use, the latex (yellow sap near the leaf skin) contains saponins and anthraquinones that are toxic to cats. Ingestion can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and lethargy.
Explore →
🐱
Anthurium
⚠️
Anthuriums are prized for their glossy, heart-shaped leaves and bright red spathes. They are toxic to cats due to calcium oxalate crystals, causing oral irritation and digestive distress.
Explore →
🐱
Azalea
⚠️
Azalea contains grayanotoxins which can cause severe vomiting, diarrhea, and cardiovascular problems in cats.
Explore →
🐱
Begonia
⚠️
Begonias contain calcium oxalate crystals that can cause intense oral irritation, drooling, and digestive upset in cats. Immediate rinsing can help alleviate discomfort.
Explore →
🐱
Bird of Paradise
⚠️
Bird of Paradise contains hydrocyanic acid which can cause gastrointestinal irritation and vomiting in cats.
Explore →
🐱
Carnations
⚠️
Carnations can cause mild gastrointestinal upset and skin irritation in cats. They’re not life-threatening, but best kept away from curious kitties.
Explore →
🐱
Chrysanthemums
⚠️
Chrysanthemums contain pyrethrins and sesquiterpene lactones that can cause vomiting, diarrhea, drooling, and even depression or incoordination in cats.
Explore →
🐱
Croton
⚠️
Crotons are known for their vibrant, colorful foliage but are toxic to cats due to calcium oxalate crystals and other compounds. Ingestion can cause oral irritation, vomiting, and digestive distress.
Explore →
🐱
Cyclamen
⚠️
Cyclamen contains cyclamine which can cause severe vomiting and diarrhea in cats.
Explore →
🐱
Daffodils
⚠️
Beloved spring flowers that can irritate a cat’s mouth and GI tract. Bulbs contain the highest toxin concentration; petals and leaves are less toxic but still irritating.
Explore →
🐱
Dieffenbachia
⚠️
Dieffenbachia contains calcium oxalate crystals which can cause severe oral irritation and swelling in cats.
Explore →
🐱
Dracaena
⚠️
Dracaena marginata, also known as the Madagascar Dragon Tree, is a hardy plant with slender stems and sword-like leaves. It is toxic to cats, potentially causing vomiting and lethargy.
Explore →
🐱
English Ivy
⚠️
English Ivy contains triterpenoid saponins that can cause oral irritation, vomiting, and diarrhea in cats. Even small ingestions may lead to discomfort.
Explore →
🐱
Fiddle Leaf Fig
⚠️
The Fiddle Leaf Fig is a trendy indoor tree with large, violin-shaped leaves. While aesthetically pleasing, it is toxic to cats, causing oral irritation, vomiting, and diarrhea if ingested.
Explore →
🐱
Geranium
⚠️
Geraniums contain geraniol and other compounds that can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and lethargy in cats. Ingestion should be avoided, though severe outcomes are rare.
Explore →
🐱
Jade Plant
⚠️
Jade plants contain compounds that can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and lethargy in cats if ingested. While not usually life-threatening, prompt veterinary attention is recommended.
Explore →
🐱
Kalanchoe
⚠️
Kalanchoe contains cardiac glycosides that can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and abnormal heart rhythm if ingested by cats. While rarely fatal, it poses a significant health risk.
Explore →
🐱
Kalanchoe
⚠️
Kalanchoe contains bufadienolides which can cause severe vomiting and heart problems in cats.
Explore →
🐱
Lilies
⚠️
All parts of lilies are extremely toxic to cats and can cause kidney failure even in small amounts.
Explore →
🐱
Lilies
⚠️
All parts of lilies are toxic to cats.
Explore →
🐱
Monstera
⚠️
Contains insoluble calcium oxalate crystals that cause intense burning and irritation of the mouth, tongue, and lips in cats. While rarely fatal, it causes significant discomfort.
Explore →
🐱
Oleander
❌
All parts contain cardiac glycosides extremely toxic to cats. Ingestion may cause severe heart problems, digestive upset, and can be fatal.
Explore →
🐱
Peace Lily
⚠️
Peace Lilies are elegant plants that produce white, sail-like flowers and dark green leaves. They are excellent at improving indoor air quality and are tolerant of low light conditions. Unfortunately, they are toxic to cats and dogs, containing calcium oxalate crystals that cause oral irritation, drooling, and vomiting.
Explore →
🐱
Philodendron
⚠️
Philodendrons are a large genus of flowering plants with attractive heart-shaped or deeply lobed leaves. They are relatively easy to care for, preferring bright, indirect light and moderate watering. These popular houseplants are toxic to cats and dogs, containing calcium oxalate crystals that can cause pain and inflammation if chewed.
Explore →
🐱
Potato Plant
⚠️
Potato plants contain solanine which can cause gastrointestinal upset and neurological symptoms in cats.
Explore →
🐱
Pothos
⚠️
Pothos, also known as Devil's Ivy, is an incredibly resilient trailing vine that thrives in a wide range of lighting conditions. It's perfect for hanging baskets or as a climbing plant with support. However, pothos is toxic to cats and dogs due to calcium oxalate crystals, which can cause oral irritation, swelling, and digestive distress.
Explore →
🐱
Rubber Plant
⚠️
Rubber Plants are popular for their glossy, dark green leaves and easy-care nature. However, they are toxic to cats, containing latex that can cause oral irritation and digestive upset.
Explore →
🐱
Sago Palm
❌
Every part is highly toxic to cats—seeds (nuts) are most deadly. Even a single seed can cause severe liver failure, seizures, and death within 24–48 hours.
Explore →
🐱
Tomato Plant
⚠️
Tomato plants contain solanine and tomatine which can cause gastrointestinal upset in cats.
Explore →
🐱
Tulips
❌
Tulips contain tulipalin A and B, which are most concentrated in the bulbs. Ingestion can cause drooling, vomiting, and diarrhea. Leaves and flowers are less toxic but still irritating.
Explore →
🐱
Yew
⚠️
Yew contains taxine which is extremely toxic to cats and can cause sudden death without warning.
Explore →
🐱
ZZ Plant
⚠️
ZZ Plants are known for their glossy, dark green leaves and remarkable ability to thrive in low light with infrequent watering. They are nearly indestructible and perfect for low-light offices or homes. However, all parts of the plant are toxic to cats and dogs, causing severe digestive issues if ingested.
Explore →
